verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Again the next day after John stood, and two of his disciples; and looking upon Jesus as He walked, he saith, Behold the Lamb of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The nature of man is somehow a thing slothful, and easily declining to perdition, not by reason of the constitution of the nature itself, but by reason of that sloth which is of deliberate choice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore it needs much reminding.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And for this cause Paul, writing to the Philippians, said, “To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not grievous, but for you it is safe.” ( Philip. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The earth when it has once received the seed, straightway gives forth its fruits, and needs not a second sowing; but with our souls it is not so, and one must be content, after having sown many times, and manifested much carefulness, to be able once to receive fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For in the first place, what is said settles in the mind with difficulty, because the ground is very hard, and entangled with thorns innumerable, and there are many which lay plots, and carry away the seed; afterwards, when it has been fixed and has taken root, it still needs the same attention, that it may come to maturity, and having done so may remain uninjured, and take no harm from any.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For in the case of seeds, when the ear is fully formed and has gained its proper strength, it easily despises rust, and drought, and every other thing; but it is not so with doctrines; in their case after all the work has been fully done, one storm and flood often comes on, and either by the attack of unpleasant circumstances, or by the plots of men skilled to deceive, or by various other temptations brought against them, brings them to ruin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I have not said this without cause, but that when you hear John repeating the same words, you may not condemn him for vain talking; nor deem him impertinent or wearisome.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He desired to have been heard by once speaking, but because not many gave heed to what was spoken from the first, by reason of deep sleep, he again rouses them by this second call.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now observe; he had said, “He that cometh after me, is preferred before me”: and that “I am not worthy to unloose the latchet of His shoe”; and that “He baptizeth with the Holy Ghost, and with fire”; and that he “saw the Spirit descending like a dove, and it abode upon Him,” and he “bare record that this is the Son of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No one gave heed, nor asked, nor said, “Why sayest thou these things? in whose behalf? for what reason?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again he had said, “Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world”; yet not even so did he touch their insensibility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, after this he is compelled to repeat the same words again, as if softening by tillage some hard and stubborn soil, and by his word as by a plow, disturbing the mind which had hardened into clods, so as to put in the seed deep.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this reason he does not make his discourse a long one either; because he desired one thing only, to bring them over and join them to Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He knew that as soon as they had received this saying, and had been persuaded, they would not afterwards need one to bear witness unto Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As also it came to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, if the Samaritans could say to the woman after hearing Him, “Now we believe, not because of thy saying, for we know that this is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world,” the disciples would be much more quickly subdued, as was the case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when they had come and heard Him but one evening, they returned no more to John, but were so nailed to Him, that they took upon them the ministry of John, and themselves proclaimed Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, saith the Evangelist, “He findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe, I pray you, this, how, when he said, “He that cometh after me is preferred before me”; and that, “I am not worthy to unloose the lachet of His shoe”; he caught no one, but when he spoke of the Dispensation, and lowered his discourse to a humbler tone, then the disciples followed Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And we may remark this, not only in the instance of the disciples, but that the many are not so much attracted when some great and sublime thing is said concerning God, as when some act of graciousness and lovingkindness, something pertaining to the salvation of the hearers, is spoken of.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They heard that “He taketh away the sin of the world,” and straightway they ran to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, said they, “if it is possible to wash away the charges that lie against us, why do we delay? here is One who will deliver us without labor of ours.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it not extreme folly to put off accepting the Gift?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let those hear who are Catechumens, and are putting off their salvation to their latest breath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Again,” saith the Evangelist, “John stood, and saith, Behold, the Lamb of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ utters no word, His messenger saith all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So it is with a bridegroom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith not for a while anything to the bride, but is there in silence, while some show him to the bride, and others give her into his hands; she merely appears, and he departs not having taken her himself, but when he has received her from another who gives her to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when he has received her thus given, he so disposes her, that she no more remembers those who betrothed her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So it was with Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He came to join to Himself the Church; He said nothing, but merely came.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was His friend, John, who put into His the bride’s right hand, when by his discourses he gave into His hand the souls of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He having received them, afterwards so disposed them, that they departed no more to John who had committed them to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And here we may remark, not this only, but something besides.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As at a marriage the maiden goes not to the bridegroom, but he hastens to her, though he be a king’s son, and though he be about to espouse some poor and abject person, or even a servant, so it was here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Man’s nature did not go up, but contemptible and poor as it was, He came to it, and when the marriage had taken place, He suffered it no longer to tarry here, but having taken it to Himself, transported it to the house of His Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then doth not John take his disciples apart, and converse with them on these matters, and so deliver them over to Christ, instead of saying publicly to them in common with all the people, ‘Behold the Lamb of God’?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That it may not seem to be a matter of arrangement; for had they gone away from him to Christ after having been privately admonished by him, and as though to do him a favor, they would perhaps soon have started away again; but now, having taken upon them the following Him, from teaching which had been general, they afterwards remained His firm disciples, as not having followed Him in order to gratify the teacher, but as looking purely to their own advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Prophets and Apostles then all preached Him absent; the Prophets before His coming according to the flesh, the Apostles after He was taken up; John alone proclaimed Him present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore he calls himself the “friend of the Bridegroom” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29 ), since he alone was present at the marriage, he it was that did and accomplished all, he made a beginning of the work.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And “looking upon Jesus walking, he saith, Behold the Lamb of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not by voice alone, but with his eyes also he bore witness to, and expressed his admiration of, Christ, rejoicing and glorying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor does he for awhile address any word of exhortation to his followers, but only shows wonder and astonishment at Him who was present, and declares to all the Gift which He came to give, and the manner of purification.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “the Lamb” declares both these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And he said not, “Who shall take,” or “Who hath taken”; but, “Who taketh away the sins of the world”; because this He ever doth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He took them not then only when He suffered, but from that time even to the present doth He take them away, not being repeatedly crucified, (for He offered One Sacrifice for sins,) but by that One continually purging them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then The Word shows us His pre-eminence, and The Son His superiority in comparison with others, so “The Lamb, The Christ, that Prophet, the True Light, the Good Shepherd,” and whatever other names are applied to Him with the addition of the article, mark a great difference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For there were many “Lambs,” and “Prophets,” and “Christs,” and “sons,” but from all these John separates Him by a wide interval.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this he secured not by the article only, but by the addition of “Only-Begotten”; for He had nothing in common with the creation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If it seems to any unseasonable that these things should be spoken at “the tenth hour” (that was the time of day, for he says, “It was about the tenth hour”—( v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 39 ), such an one seems to me to be much mistaken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the case indeed of the many, and those who serve the flesh, the season after feasting is not very suitable for any matters of pressing moment, because their hearts are burdened with meats: but here was a man who did not even partake of common food, and who at evening was as sober as we are at morning, (or rather much more so; for often the remains of our evening food that are left within us, fill our souls with imaginations, but he loaded his vessel with none of these things;) he with good reason spake late in the evening of these matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, he was tarrying in the wilderness by Jordan, where all came to his baptism with great fear, and caring little at that time for the things of this life; as also they continued with Christ three days, and had nothing to eat. ( Matt. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the part of a zealous herald and a careful husbandman, not to desist before he see that the planted seed has got a firm hold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then did he not go about all the parts of Judæa preaching Christ, rather than stand by the river waiting for Him to come, that he might point Him out when He came?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because he wished that this should be effected by His works; his own object being in the mean time only to make Him known, and to persuade some to hear of eternal life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But to Him he leaves the greater testimony, that of works, as also He saith, “I receive not testimony of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The works which My Father hath given Me, the same bear witness of Me.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how much more effectual this was; for when he had thrown in a little spark, at once the blaze rose on high.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they who before had not even given heed to his words, afterwards say, “All things which John spake were true.” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, if he had gone about saying these things, what was being done would have seemed to be done from some human motive, and the preaching to be full of suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And the two disciples heard him, and followed Jesus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet John had other disciples, but they not only did not “follow Jesus,” but were even jealously disposed towards him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Rabbi,” says one, “He that was with thee beyond Jordan, to whom thou barest witness, behold, the same baptizeth, and all men come unto him.” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again they appear bringing a charge against him; “Why do we fast, but thy disciples fast not?” ( Matt. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But those who were better than the rest had no such feeling, but heard, and at once followed; followed, not as despising their teacher, but as being most fully persuaded by him, and producing the strongest proof that they acted thus from a right judgment of his reasonings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they did not do so by his advice, that might have appeared suspicious; but when he merely foretold what was to come to pass, that “He should baptize with the Holy Ghost, [and with fire,]” they followed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They did not then desert their teacher, but rather desired to learn what Christ brought with Him more than John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe zeal combined with modesty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They did not at once approach and question Jesus on necessary and most important matters, nor were they desirous to converse with Him publicly, while all were present, at once and in an off-hand manner, but privately; for they knew that the words of their teacher proceeded not from humility, but from truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “One of the two who heard, and followed Him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore then has he not made known the name of the other also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some say, because it was the writer himself that followed; others, not so, but that he was not one of the distinguished disciples; it behooved not therefore to say more than was necessary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what would it have advantaged us to learn his name, when the writer does not mention the names even of the seventy-two?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: St. Paul also did the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We have sent,” says he, “with him the brother,” (who has often in many things been forward,) “whose praise is in the Gospel.” ( 2 Cor. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, he mentions Andrew for another reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is, that when you are informed that Simon having in company with him heard, “Follow Me, and I will make you fishers of men” ( Matt. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ), was not perplexed at so strange a promise, you may learn that his brother had already laid down within him the beginnings of the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then Jesus turned, and saw them following, and saith unto them, What seek ye?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hence we are taught, that God does not prevent our wills by His gifts, but that when we begin, when we provide the being willing, then He gives us many opportunities of salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What seek ye?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He who knoweth the hearts of men, who dwelleth in our thoughts, doth He ask?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He doth; not that He may be informed; how could that be? but that by the question He may make them more familiar, and impart to them greater boldness, and show them that they are worthy to hear Him; for it was probable that they would blush and be afraid, as being unknown to him, and as having heard such accounts of Him from the testimony of their teacher.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore to remove all this, their shame and their fear, he questions them, and would not let them come all the way to the house in silence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the event would have been the same had He not questioned them; they would have remained by following Him, and walking in His steps would have reached His dwelling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then did He ask?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To effect that which I said, to calm their minds, yet disturbed with shame and anxiety, and to give them confidence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor was it by their following only that they showed their earnest desire, but by their question also: for when they had not as yet learned or even heard anything from Him, they call Him, “Master”; thrusting themselves as it were among His disciples, and declaring what was the cause of their following, that they might hear somewhat profitable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe their wisdom also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They did not say, “Teach us of Thy doctrines, or some other thing that we need to know”; but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Where dwellest Thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because, as I before said, they wished in quiet to say somewhat to Him, and to hear somewhat from Him, and to learn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore they did not defer the matter, nor say, “We will come to-morrow by all means, and hear thee speak in public”; but showed the great eagerness they had to hear Him, by not being turned back even by the hour, for the sun was already near its setting, (“it was,” saith John, “about the tenth hour.”)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore Christ does not tell them the marks of His abode, nor its situation, but rather induces them to follow Him by showing them that He had accepted them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this reason He did not say anything of this kind to them, “It is an unseasonable time now for you to enter into the house, to-morrow you shall hear if you have any wish, return home now”; but converses with them as with friends, and those who had long been with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith He in another place, “But the Son of Man hath not where to lay His head” ( Luke ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 58 ), while here He saith, “Come and see” ( v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 39 ) where I abide?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because the expression “hath not where to lay His head,” signifies that He had no dwelling place of His own, not that He did not abide in a house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this too is the meaning of the comparison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Evangelist has mentioned that “they abode with Him that day,” but has not added wherefore, because the reason was plain; for from no other motive did they follow Christ, and He draw them to Him, but only that they might have instruction; and this they enjoyed so abundantly and eagerly even in a single night, that they both proceeded straightway to the capture of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then also learn hence to consider all things secondary to the hearing the word of God, and to deem no season unseasonable, and, though a man may even have to go into another person’s house, and being a person unknown to make himself known to great men, though it be late in the day, or at any time whatever, never to neglect this traffic.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let food and baths and dinners and the other things of this life have their appointed time; but let the teaching of heavenly philosophy have no separate time, let every season belong to it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Paul saith, “In season, out of season, reprove, rebuke, exhort” ( 2 Tim. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2 ); and the Prophet too saith, “In His law will he meditate day and night” ( Ps. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ); and Moses commanded the Jews to do this always.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the things of this life, baths, I mean, and dinners, even if they are necessary, yet being continually repeated, render the body feeble; but the teaching of the soul the more it is prolonged, the stronger it renders the soul which receives it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now we portion out all our time for trifles and unprofitable silly talking, and we sit together idly during the morning and afternoon, midday and evening besides, and we have appointed places for this; but hearing the divine doctrines twice or thrice in the week we become sick, and thoroughly sated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is the reason?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We are in a bad state of soul; its faculty of desiring and reaching after these things we have relaxed altogether.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore it is not strong enough to have an appetite for spiritual food.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this among others is a great proof of weakness, not to hunger nor thirst, but to be disinclined to both.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if this, when it takes place in our bodies, is a sure sign of grievous disease, and productive of weakness, much more is it so in the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then,” says one, “shall we be able to renew it, thus fallen and relaxed, to strength? what doing, what saying?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By applying ourselves to the divine words of the prophets, of the Apostles, of the Gospels, and all the others; then we shall know that it is far better to feed on these than on impure food, for so we must term our unseasonable idle talking and assemblies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For which is best, tell me, to converse on things relating to the market, or things in the law courts, or in the camp, or on things in heaven, and on what shall be after our departure hence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which is best, to talk about our neighbor and our neighbor’s affairs, to busy ourselves in what belongs to other people, or to enquire into the things of angels, and into matters which concern ourselves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a neighbor’s affairs are not thine at all; but heavenly things are thine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” says some one, “a man may by once speaking finish these subjects altogether.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why do you not think this in matters on which you converse uselessly and idly, why though ye waste your lives on this have ye never exhausted the subject?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And I have not yet named what is far more vile than this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These are the things about which the better sort converse one with the other; but the more indifferent and careless carry about in their talk players and dancers and charioteers, defiling men’s ears, corrupting their souls, and driving their nature into mad excesses by these narratives, and by means of this discourse introducing every kind of wickedness into their own imagination.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as soon as the tongue has uttered the name of the dancer, immediately the soul has figured to itself his looks, his hair, his delicate clothing, and himself more effeminate than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Another again fans the flame in another way, by introducing some harlot into the conversation, with her words, and attitudes, and glances, her languishing looks and twisted locks, the smoothness of her cheeks, and her painted eyelids.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Were you not somewhat affected when I gave this description?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet be not ashamed, nor blush, for the very necessity of nature requires this, and so disposes the soul according as the tendency of what is said may be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if, when it is I that speak, you, standing in the church, and at a distance from these things, were somewhat affected at the hearing, consider how it is likely that they are disposed, who sit in the theater itself, who are totally free from dread, who are absent from this venerable and awful assembly, who both see and hear those things with much shamelessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And why then,” perhaps one of those who heed not may say, “if the necessity of nature so disposes the soul, do you let go that, and blame us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because, to be softened when one hears these things, is nature’s work; but to hear them is not a fault of nature, but of deliberate choice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For so he who meddles with fire must needs be injured, so wills the weakness of our nature; yet nature does not therefore draw us to the fire and to the injury thence arising; this can be only from deliberate perversity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I beseech you, therefore, to remove and correct this fault, that you may not of your own accord cast yourself down the precipice, nor thrust yourselves into the pits of wickedness, nor run of yourselves to the blaze, lest we place ourselves in jeopardy of the fire prepared for the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: May it come to pass, that we all being delivered both from this fire and from that, may go to the very bosom of Abraham, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and Holy Ghost, be glory for ever and ever.
